title: [BUG] Excel is not generated correctly if the name in AddTable contains a ```hyphen(-)```
author: ramiKang
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report
Lib version: 4.3.0

## Steps To Reproduce

### 1. Write code
```javascript

...

workSheet.addTable({
  name: "test-name",
  ref: `testRef`,
  headerRow: true,
  totalsRow: false,
  columns: testTableHeader,
  rows: testTableRows,
});

...
```

### 2. DownLoad Excel

### 3. Open Excel by Microsoft 365
The following message appears:

**Korean** : (파일 제목).xlsx의 내용에 문제가 있습니다. 이 통합 문서의 내용을 최대한 복구하시겠습니까? 이 통합 문서의 원본을 신뢰하는 경우 [예]를 클릭하세요.

**English** : We found a problem with some content in '<filename>.xlsx'. Do you want us to try to recover as much as we can? If you trust the source of this workbook, click Yes.


![스크린샷 2024-10-30 오후 6 41 20](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e800a734-c5b4-4f0a-8266-535316b58d81)


## The expected behaviour:

Please fix the issue to ensure that the Excel file can be opened correctly even if the name field contains a hyphen.

## ETC
If the Excel file does not open correctly when using the `addTable` function, try modifying the name field to `camelCase`.

comments.author: fadingNA
comments.body: Can I work on this issue?

comments.author: ramiKang
fadingNA
comments.body: @ramiKang  got it let me do that
